briefdescription.para: Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
XAudioFormatter_LookupConfig returns a reference to an XAudioFormatter_Config structure based on the unique device id, DeviceId.

detaileddescription.para: The return value will refer to an entry in the device configuration table defined in the xaudioformatter_g.c file.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the device for the lookup operation.

simplesect.para: CfgPtr is a reference to a config record in the configuration table (in xaudioformatter_g.c) corresponding to DeviceId, or NULL if no match is found.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the audio formatter core instance.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAudioFormatter instance.
is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific XAudioFormatter instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the interrupts for audio formatter core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAudioFormatter instance.
is the interrupt mask.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the interrupts for audio formatter core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAudioFormatter instance.
is the interrupt mask.

briefdescription.para: This function starts the dma for audio formatter core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAudioFormatter instance.

briefdescription.para: This function stops the dma for audio formatter core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAudioFormatter instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the hw params for audio formatter core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAudioFormatter instance.
is a pointer to the XAudioFormatterHwParams structure which contains the no. of channels, bit width, no. of periods, bytes per period, bytes per channel and buffer address.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the interrupts for audio formatter core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAudioFormatter instance.
is the interrupt mask.

briefdescription.para: This function soft resets the audio formatter DMA core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAudioFormatter instance.

briefdescription.para: This function calculates the Fs multiplier value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Audio Formatter instance.
is the frequency of the MMclk.
is the sampling frequency of the system.

detaileddescription.para: of bytes of data read from memory..

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Audio Formatter instance.

simplesect.para: - No. of bytes of data read from memory.

briefdescription.para: This function reads all the Channel Status registers and writes to a buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Audio Formatter instance.
is a pointer to a buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the S2MM timeout.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Audio Formatter instance.
is the Timeout value after which all the data is flushed to memory if there is no incoming data from any channel.

detaileddescription.para: The device driver enables higher layer software (e.g., an application) to communicate to the Audio Formatter core.

XAudioFormatter_CfgInitialize() API is used to initialize the Audio Formatter core. The user needs to first call the XAudioFormatter_LookupConfig() API which returns the Configuration structure pointer which is passed as a parameter to the XAudioFormatter_CfgInitialize() API.
Interrupts The driver does the interrupt handling, and dispatch to the user application through callback functions that user has registered.
This driver supports Virtual Memory. The RTOS is responsible for calculating the correct device base address in Virtual Memory space.
This driver is not thread safe. Any needs for threads or thread mutual exclusion must be satisfied by the layer above this driver.

Asserts are used within all Xilinx drivers to enforce constraints on argument values. Asserts can be turned off on a system-wide basis by defining, at compile time, the NDEBUG identifier. By default, asserts are turned on and it is recommended that users leave asserts on during development.
